attacking_style: Launches solo attacks 10-15km from finish on descents or after key climbs, relying on time trial power to sustain breakaways. On flatter terrain, attacks immediately from the gun or in final 200m sprints when positioning allows.
team_tactics: Jumbo-Visma employs him as dual-threat weapon: opening domestique for GC leaders (water carrying, early attacks, echelon driving) then releases him for personal ambitions (green jersey points, stage wins) once team objectives secured. Minimal leadout train—relies on LAPORTE positioning or self-sufficiency.
terrain_preference: Excels on uphill sprint finishes after hard stages (8000ft+ climbing days), selective finishes with 3-15% gradients, and technical descents. Cobbles and gravel suit his bike handling. Struggles on sustained steep climbs (La Redoute, Kwaremont-Paterberg) when repeated at high intensity.
vulnerability: Dropped on decisive climbs when attacks occur on 15%+ gradients after multiple categorized ascents. Poor sprint positioning when isolated (frequently second place 2022). Premature celebrations and pacing errors in time trials. Vulnerable to mechanicals in critical moments (Paris-Roubaix 2019, 2023).
key_patterns: Wins time trials as stated season objective after sacrificing earlier chances for team duties (2021-2022 Tour pattern)
Enters breakaways personally to contest intermediate sprints for green jersey, even on mountain stages (4+ instances 2022 Tour)
Dominates after high tempo/crosswind work, converting diesel engine efforts into sprint wins (Dauphine/Tour 2020-2022)
Bridges solo to front groups when isolated, relying on exceptional endurance over team support (Bretagne 2022, Strade 2019)
Frequently second in sprints despite superior power, suggesting positioning or timing deficits (Tour 2022 Stages 2-4)
Dropped consistently on Oude Kwaremont-Paterberg combo when rivals attack (Flanders 2021, 2023; E3 advantages when controlling)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 showed diminished finishing speed (Tour de Suisse 3rd at 310m), dropped in Flanders despite strongest team, abandoned Tour early. Post-2022 peak decline evident.
built_from: 2021 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
2023 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
